title: Tesla found new buyers for its Cybertruck: Kazakh emergency services
summary:
Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Emergency Situations has confirmed it will purchase additional Tesla Cybertrucks after the electric pickup proved effective in rescue operations in Almaty, the country’s largest city.

The Central Asian country has become the latest small international market to adopt the Cybertruck, as Tesla continues to struggle with collapsing domestic demand for the electric pickup.

more…

title: Orange EV scores record, 600 unit order as demand for electric semi trucks soars
summary:
How do we know EVs are here to stay? Electric terminal brand Orange EV just announced a new, record single order that includes 600 new electric Class 8 trucks, proving that there’s a genuine market for HDEVs even without Federal incentives.

more…

title: Interior Dept.’s $1B bribe to a foreign oil company gets challenged in court
summary:
New York state has announced a lawsuit against the Interior Department’s illegal $1 billion payment to an oil company to stop development of a wind farm off the New York Coast.

The project would have saved New Yorkers $10 billion and created 1,700 jobs, which would have conflicted with the Department of the Interior’s goals of raising your energy costs.

more…

title: The 2027 Dodge Charger EV goes on sale with a NACS port, but it’s not cheap
summary:
Dodge opened orders for the 670-hp 2027 Charger Daytona EV, the “quickest and most powerful AWD muscle car,” but is it worth the price?

more…

title: BYD says its cast aluminum frame is lighter, tougher, and safer than steel
summary:
If you’re from Pittsburgh, you may want to sit down for this one: global EV sales leader BYD claims the aluminum frame that underpins its new Yangwang U8L SUV passes a brutal, 12 ton lift test – despite weighing over 100 lbs. less than a comparable steel frame.

more…

title: This California city is adding off-grid solar + storage EV chargers
summary:
The City of Long Beach, California, is adding six more off-grid solar EV chargers from Beam Global as it continues electrifying its vehicle fleet.

more…

title: Electrify America is changing how you pay for EV charging
summary:
Electrify America is changing how EV drivers pay for charging sessions, and it’s ditching one of the more confusing parts of its app in the process.

more…

title: Lectric just launched a new e-bike company, and it could shake up the premium market
summary:
When a new e-bike company launches, it’s usually not a huge deal. The industry sees new brands appear all the time, many of them little more than a logo slapped onto an existing bike from a Chinese catalog.

But Monarc isn’t just another startup hoping to make a splash – it’s all but guaranteed to set off a tsunami.

more…

title: Anker launches SOLIX S2000 power station + bundles starting from exclusive $598, EGO Z6 zero turn riding mower bundle new low, more
summary:
We’ve got some exciting Green Deals in our Tuesday edition this week, starting with Anker’s newly launched SOLIX S2000 Portable Power Station + bundles that start from $598 using our exclusive code (beating the standard $680 launch pricing). From there, we also spotted EGO’s Z6 56V 42-inch Cordless Electric Zero Turn Riding Mower with 6x batteries and an 880W wall charger hitting a new $4,499 low, as well as Amazon continuing the May pricing on Segway’s Max G2 and Max G3 Electric Scooters starting from $700, the EcoFlow 1,024Wh DELTA 3 Classic Portable Power Station at $445, the best price since September on Greenworks’ compact 1,800 PSI Electric Pressure Washer, and much more waiting for you below. We also have all the hangover deals collected down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s EcoFlow Hurricane Season Prep Sale lineup with up to 61% discounts, the rare return of EGO’s 3,200 PSI Electric Pressure Washer $699 low, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Subaru’s new EVs are already outselling the Solterra
summary:
Just two months after arriving at dealerships, Subaru’s new electric SUVs are outselling the Solterra.

title: The Rolls-Royce Spectre Gets Big Update. But You Wouldn’t Know It
summary: It now goes farther, charges faster, and responds more sharply, but the design remains mostly untouched.

title: The Company That Builds The iPhone Just Unveiled A Tesla Model Y Competitor
summary: It’s no Apple Car, but Foxconn’s Cavira gets an LFP battery and a dual-motor powertrain that can deliver up to 468 horsepower.

title: Tesla Is Selling Discounted Used Cybertrucks Now
summary: The cheapest used Cybertruck in Tesla’s inventory is less than $67,000. Is that enough to convince buyers, though?

title: Lexus Cancels Next-Generation EV Sedan
summary: Lexus was developing a production version of the dramatic LF-ZC electric sedan concept, an IS EV, but it’s abandoning the project.

title: Tesla Could Build A Model 3 Plaid. That Doesn’t Mean It Will
summary: Tesla’s top engineer says he thinks about a tri-motor Model 3 “all the time,” but the company’s priorities lie elsewhere.

title: Rivian Says Fight Over Apple CarPlay Is 'Completely Obsolete' Thanks To AI
summary: Rivian says the tremendous progress of AI-powered voice assistants will make CarPlay a thing of the past.

title: Orders For Slate’s Sub-$30K Bare-Bones EV Truck Finally Open On  June 24—But There’s A Catch
summary: The company is taking $300 non-refundable deposits to secure a place in line for the first finished vehicles.

title: Elon Musk Promised 1,000 Texas Robotaxis Last Year. It’s Nowhere Near That
summary: The official count was revealed for the first time in registration information submitted by Tesla to the Texas DMV.

title: It's Official: Rivian Will Start Delivering R2s In Two Weeks
summary: Order books open and test drives start in just two weeks.

title: China Is Turning Against The EV Gimmicks It Helped Popularize
summary: Hidden handles, giant screens, yokes, and reclining seats make Chinese EVs feel futuristic. Now regulators are questioning their safety.
